steps.title: Find the tense anchor
steps.content:
The previous sentence uses the simple present tense: "economists in India...use an Ease of Living Index." The blank describes what that tool does — a general, ongoing fact. General facts about a currently-used tool require simple present tense to match the established time frame.
  • Choice A — measures: Simple present tense. Matches "use" and correctly describes an ongoing property of the tool. ✓
  • Choice B — had measured: Past perfect — implies the measuring is finished and prior to another past event. The tool is still in use, so past tense is wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — would have measured: Future perfect conditional — expresses a hypothetical or unrealized future action. The tool currently exists and is used, not hypothetical. ✗
  • Choice D — will have been measuring: Future perfect continuous — describes an action that will be ongoing up to a future point. This shifts to the future with no justification in the passage. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only simple present "measures" matches the tense established by "use" and correctly describes a current, ongoing fact about the tool. A is correct.
